description: Golden test set for FundScope RAGAS evaluation. 50 PE fund domain questions across categories.
note: expected_answer fields contain placeholder values. Populate with ground-truth answers after ingesting real PE fund documents.

questions.question: Hello, what can you help me with?
questions.expected_answer: I can help you analyze PE fund documents including LPAs, side letters, and term sheets. I provide term extraction, risk analysis, obligation tracking, and answer questions about your fund documents.

questions.question: What is carried interest in private equity?
questions.expected_answer: Carried interest is the share of profits that the general partner receives as compensation, typically 20% of net profits above a preferred return hurdle rate.

questions.question: Explain the difference between European and American waterfall structures.
questions.expected_answer: In a European (whole-fund) waterfall, carry is calculated on aggregate fund returns after all invested capital is returned. In an American (deal-by-deal) waterfall, carry is calculated on each individual investment's returns.

questions.question: What does MFN mean in the context of side letters?
questions.expected_answer: MFN (Most Favored Nation) gives an LP the right to elect to receive any more favorable terms granted to other LPs in their side letters, subject to certain exclusions.

questions.question: What are typical management fee ranges for PE funds?
questions.expected_answer: Typical management fees for PE funds range from 1.5% to 2.0% of committed capital during the investment period, often stepping down to 1.0%-1.5% of invested capital during the harvest period.
